betterquesting:10.desc:8: Energy is transported through wires using ‘amps,’ which can be thought of as packets of power. All singleblock generators generate 1 amp of power per tick, the voltage of which changes based on the tier of the generator. A LV steam turbine generates 1 amp of LV power, which is 32 EU/t, whereas a MV steam turbine generates 1 amp of MV power, or 128 EU/t. MV cables can carry LV amps, but LV cables can’t carry MV amps (they will burn.)
